framework.name: OAuth 2.0 Token-Based Authorization Architecture
framework.description: Open-standard authorization framework using access tokens with defined scopes instead of credential sharing. Separates resource owner, client, authorization server, and resource server into distinct roles with token-mediated access control. Tokens can be scoped to specific resources and revoked via expiration or refresh mechanisms.
framework.raw_quote: OAuth is an authorization protocol that is based on access tokens. Access tokens are pieces of information that determine the specific resources an application is allowed to access.
